"**Morpheus**" **Maiorino** (August 16, 1979 – December 23, 2012) was a Brazilian professional [kickboxer](/wiki/Kickboxer "Kickboxer") and [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist"). He was a three time Brazilian [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight "Heavyweight") [Muay Thai](/wiki/Muay_Thai "Muay Thai") Champion, the [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1") Brazil 2004 Tournament finalist and champion,[[1]](#cite_note-Fighters_Profile-1) and a World Muay Thai Association (WMA) Super Heavyweight World Champion.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**Eduardo** "**Morpheus**" **Maiorino** (August 16, 1979 – December 23, 2012) was a Brazilian professional [kickboxer](/wiki/Kickboxer "Kickboxer") and [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist"). He was a three time Brazilian [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight "Heavyweight") [Muay Thai](/wiki/Muay_Thai "Muay Thai") Champion, the [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1") Brazil 2004 Tournament finalist and champion,[[1]](#cite_note-Fighters_Profile-1) and a World Muay Thai Association (WMA) Super Heavyweight World Champion.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Biography and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eduardo_Maiorino&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography and career")]

Eduardo Maiorino de Morais was born in [Campo Grande](/wiki/Campo_Grande "Campo Grande"), [Mato Grosso do Sul](/wiki/Mato_Grosso_do_Sul "Mato Grosso do Sul"), Brazil and died December 23, 2012, after a heart attack.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was 33.

## Titles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eduardo_Maiorino&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Titles")]

* 2011 WKBC World Champion (Heavyweight division).
* 2010 2nd place in World Championship WMA (World Muay Thai Association) Hangzhou, China.
* 2010 10th place in world rankings in WBC Muay Thai (super cruiserweight division).
* 2009 3rd in rankings IMC International Muay Thai Council.
* 2009 World Champion (Super Heavyweight division) World Kickboxing League.
* 2009 World Heavyweight Champ (WKL).
* 2008 State champion in Jiu-Jitsu.
* 2007 Brazilian Muay thai champion.
* 2007 Brazilian Champion of Demolition Fight III Muay Thai.
* 2006 Brazilian Vale Tudo tournament champion.
* Three times Brazilian Muay Thai Champion C.B.M.T "Brazilian Muay Thai Confederation" (2005, 2004, 2003).
* 2004 Vitória Extreme Fight MMA Grand Prix Champion.
* [2004 K-1 Brazil Challenge Champion](/wiki/K-1_Brazil_2004_Challenge "K-1 Brazil 2004 Challenge").
* 2004 WKN Brazilian Muay Thai Champion.
* 2004 Champion of K-1 Brazil Grand Prix in Goiânia-GO.
* 2003 3rd World M-1 Kickboxing GP in Venice-ITA.
* 2003 2nd place in K-1 Brazil Grand Prix (São Paulo-SP).
* 2003–05 Brazilian Muay Thai Confederation Champion.
* [2003 K-1 World Grand Prix Preliminary Brazil runner up](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2003_Preliminary_Brazil "K-1 World Grand Prix 2003 Preliminary Brazil")[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* 2002 Centro-Oeste Boxing champion.
